tableRow.maxContractTerm: Five (5) years; ten (10) years if the contract includes transportation provided by at least one zero-emission school bus (as defined in 42 USC 16091(a)(8)).
tableRow.rebidRequired: Not required by state statute. No Connecticut statute mandates competitive bidding for school district pupil-transportation contracts; CGS 7-148v permits (does not compel) municipal competitive-bidding ordinances. At term-end a new contract is required (because CGS 10-220(a) caps each contract at 5/10 years), but the procurement method is a local choice unless local charter/ordinance directs otherwise.
tableRow.renewalMechanics: Statute silent. CGS 10-220(a) caps each single award at 5 (or 10) years and establishes no automatic rollover, evergreen, CPI/index adjustment, fuel escalator, or board/agency-approval renewal mechanism beyond that cap. At term-end, a new contract is required.
tableRow.statutoryCitation: Conn. Gen. Stat. Sec. 10-220(a); 2022 Conn. Pub. Acts No. 22-25, Sec. 12; CGS Sec. 7-148v.
tableRow.districtVariance: Unknown — no credible evidence of differing statutory interpretation of CGS 10-220(a) located. Variance across districts is in procurement practice, not in statutory interpretation.

sourcesReVerified: CT SDE School Accommodations Workshop Package (Oct. 2008)
note: Full per-state prose lives in research-findings.md, source-ledger.md, verification-notes.md, and challenge-matrix.md.
